item.text: Baseline is confirmed: level 3 completed in 76 moves, so the harness reaches level 4 but has no level-4 player. Next I’m inspecting only the indexed player/leg definitions and the documented perception constants/API needed for bounded clone probes.

item.text: The one-step experiment identifies action 2 as moving a three-row horizontal scanner downward; action 5 changes scanner markers and part of the upper-left object, while 1/3/4 do nothing before selection. This matches the earlier select–move–validate mechanic, so I’m now testing action 5 followed by each direction and comparing object geometry against the two right-side silhouettes.

The dense subgoals are now verified: the seven-cell U piece overlays the U subset of the upper target after seven right moves, and the six-cell bar overlays the bar subset of the lower target after seven right moves. After deselecting and sweeping the scanner downward, the real reward increases to level 4. I’m narrowing the sweep length, then I’ll encode the sequence solely with the existing reusable `repeat_action` leg and validate the full replay.

The implemented level-4 player succeeds end to end: `RESULT levels=4 moves=102 replay_ok=True err=None`. I’m removing the temporary probe script I created so the workspace is left with only the solver change; the shared leg library remains unchanged because `repeat_action` already expresses every required skill.
